title: digitalis mauretanica
intro: Digitalis mauretanica, also known as North African foxglove, is a flowering plant belonging to the foxglove family (Scrophulariaceae) and is native to North Africa. This plant contains cardenolides, which are medicinal compounds with similar properties to those found in Digitalis purpurea, the common foxglove. The medicinal properties of Digitalis mauretanica include its ability to increase heart rate and contractility, making it a potential treatment for heart failure and other cardiac conditions. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations to support cardiovascular health, and can be consumed in the form of tea, tincture, or extract, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity.
